paper_metadata.title: Green Taxes in a Post-Paris World: Are Millions of Nays Inevitable?
paper_metadata.authors: Stefano Carattini; Andrea Baranzini; Philippe Thalmann; Frédéric Varone; Frank Vöhringer
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: VOX survey after Swiss 2015 energy-tax initiative ballot (N≈1500); plus choice experiment (N=1200) administered March-May 2015 on representative Swiss population.
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Studies acceptability of a Swiss referendum on replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy, and a full-information choice experiment for a carbon tax (CHF/tCO2) with alternative revenue recycling designs.

determinants.definition: Agreement that implementing the energy tax would harm firms’ competitiveness (e.g., higher costs and industry losses).
determinants.mechanism: Perceived competitiveness losses reduce support for the energy/carbon tax reform.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient −0.394**; marginal effect −0.014**.
determinants.notes: Core opposition argument in the real ballot.

determinants.definition: Agreement that the energy-tax proposal would have regressive distributional impacts, burdening lower-income households more.
determinants.mechanism: Perceived unfair distribution lowers support for the tax reform.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient −0.165*; marginal effect −0.006*.
determinants.notes: Distributional concerns reduce the probability of voting yes.

determinants.definition: Agreement that the energy tax would create incentives to save energy and switch to cleaner/renewable sources.
determinants.mechanism: Belief in environmental effectiveness increases the probability of voting yes.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient 0.378***; marginal effect 0.013**.
determinants.notes: Perceived environmental effectiveness is a strong positive predictor of support.

determinants.definition: Agreement that the initiative is revenue neutral (overall tax burden unchanged).
determinants.mechanism: Perceived fiscal neutrality reduces resistance by alleviating fears of net tax hikes.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient 0.617***; marginal effect 0.022**.
determinants.notes: Higher agreement with revenue neutrality increases likelihood of a yes vote.

determinants.definition: Agreement that the energy tax would increase energy security by reducing dependence on oil/gas exporters.
determinants.mechanism: Perceived co-benefits raise support for the proposed energy tax.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient 0.181*; marginal effect 0.007*.

determinants.definition: Household car ownership, proxy for sensitivity to higher fuel/energy prices from the proposed energy tax.
determinants.mechanism: More cars imply greater expected fuel-cost burden, reducing support.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient −0.457**; marginal effect −0.016**.

determinants.definition: Concern about public intervention in the economy (anti-government-intervention attitude).
determinants.mechanism: Greater skepticism toward state intervention reduces support for tax-based reform.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient −0.085*; marginal effect −0.003*.
determinants.notes: Ideological opposition to intervention via taxes reduces support.

determinants.definition: Declared affinity/support for green parties (Green Liberals/Green Party).
determinants.mechanism: Ideological alignment increases the likelihood of voting yes.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Swiss Green Liberal popular initiative replacing VAT with an energy tax on non-renewable energy (vote March 8, 2015).
determinants.outcome_variable: Yes-vote on the initiative (probit among ballot participants).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Probit incl. pros/cons: coefficient 0.628**; marginal effect 0.022**.

determinants.definition: Carbon tax level (CHF per ton CO2) in the choice experiment (0 vs 60/90/120/150).
determinants.mechanism: Higher rates raise expected energy prices and costs, lowering acceptability.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Choice experiment: carbon tax at 60–150 CHF/tCO2 with specified revenue recycling options vs 0 CHF status quo.
determinants.outcome_variable: Acceptability of proposed carbon-tax designs in the choice experiment.
determinants.evidence_quote: As expected, a higher tax rate is linked to a lower acceptability, everything else equal.
determinants.notes: Support falls sharply for rates ≥90 CHF/tCO2.

determinants.definition: Use of carbon tax revenues for lump-sum transfers to households.
determinants.mechanism: Progressive transfers offset distributional burdens, increasing carbon-tax acceptability.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Choice experiment: carbon tax with revenue recycling options (income-tax rebates, VAT reduction, lump-sum transfers, social cushioning, environmental earmarking).
determinants.outcome_variable: Acceptability of proposed carbon-tax designs in the choice experiment.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Conditional logit (ref income-tax rebate): marginal effect +0.137*** (SE 0.012).
determinants.notes: Lump-sum transfers are strongly preferred when distributional impacts are made salient.

determinants.definition: Use of carbon tax revenues for social cushioning/social benefits to protect households from burdens.
determinants.mechanism: Mitigates regressive energy-price impacts, increasing acceptability.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Choice experiment: carbon tax with revenue recycling options including social cushioning.
determinants.outcome_variable: Acceptability of proposed carbon-tax designs in the choice experiment.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Conditional logit (ref income-tax rebate): marginal effect +0.144*** (SE 0.011).
determinants.notes: Distributional protection via social benefits raises support.

determinants.definition: Use of carbon tax revenues to purchase international carbon offsets/additional emission abatements (environmental earmarking).
determinants.mechanism: With emissions effectiveness already communicated, offset-based earmarking loses appeal; some may also dislike foreign credits.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Choice experiment: carbon tax with environmental earmarking via purchase of international offsets.
determinants.outcome_variable: Acceptability of proposed carbon-tax designs in the choice experiment.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Conditional logit: full sample −0.021 (ns); nationals −0.029*; without no-voters −0.035**; always voters −0.056**.
determinants.notes: Environmental recycling is not popular, especially once voters are informed about effectiveness.
